title: Music
subject: Music
description: Learn about rhythm, instruments, and Jamaican music
curriculum: Based on early music education standards with Jamaican cultural focus

sections.name: Rhythm and Beat
sections.instruction: Music has a beat - like a heartbeat! Learn about rhythm.

questions.prompt: What is the steady sound in music called?
questions.hint: Clap your hands to the ___ of the music!

questions.prompt: When music is FAST, the beat is:
questions.hint: Fast music has a quick, speedy beat!

questions.prompt: When you clap in time with music, you are keeping the:
questions.hint: Clap, clap, clap to the ___!

questions.prompt: A LULLABY is usually:
questions.hint: A lullaby helps babies fall asleep!

sections.name: Musical Instruments
sections.instruction: Learn about different types of instruments!

questions.prompt: Which instrument do you HIT to make sound?
questions.hint: You use sticks or your hands to play this!

questions.prompt: Which instrument has strings you pluck or strum?
questions.hint: Reggae musicians often play this!

questions.prompt: Which instrument do you BLOW into?
questions.hint: Wind instruments need your breath!

questions.prompt: What family does the DRUM belong to?
questions.hint: Percussion instruments are hit, shaken, or scraped!

questions.prompt: Which is a Jamaican percussion instrument made from bamboo?
questions.hint: This instrument is used in mento music!

sections.name: Jamaican Music
sections.instruction: Jamaica has special types of music. Let's learn about them!

questions.prompt: What type of music is Jamaica MOST famous for around the world?
questions.hint: Bob Marley made this music famous!

questions.prompt: Who is known as the 'King of Reggae'?
questions.hint: He sang 'One Love' and 'Three Little Birds'!

questions.prompt: What is the oldest form of Jamaican popular music?
questions.hint: This style came before reggae and ska!

questions.prompt: What song do Jamaicans often sing at Independence celebrations?
questions.hint: This is one of Jamaica's national songs!

sections.name: Singing and Voice
sections.instruction: Your voice is an instrument too!

questions.prompt: When you sing LOUDLY, your voice is:
questions.hint: Loud singing needs a strong voice!

questions.prompt: When you sing a HIGH note, your voice goes:
questions.hint: High notes are like going up a ladder!

questions.prompt: What are the WORDS in a song called?
questions.hint: These are the words you sing!

questions.prompt: When many people sing together, it's called a:
questions.hint: Churches often have these singing groups!

questions.prompt: What is Jamaica's National Anthem called?
questions.hint: We sing this at important national events!

questions.prompt: When should you STAND during the National Anthem?
questions.hint: We show respect by standing!

questions.prompt: The first words of Jamaica's anthem are:
questions.hint: The anthem starts as a prayer!
